title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited by their inability to maintain context across interactions and see the full codebase, comparing them to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He advocates for architectural improvements to enable greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx argues that today's coding agents behave like a brilliant engineer with amnesia: capable in the moment, but unable to retain context across interactions or see the full codebase. The talk frames these as architectural limitations rather than model failures, pointing to the context window and the absence of persistent agent memory as the core constraints. Savkin advocates for structural improvements, including monorepo organization, to give agents broader visibility and the memory needed for greater autonomy in software development.
